compiled_state.title: As a developer, config key names in onboarding match validate-config.sh KNOWN_KEYS
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Correct key name mismatches in onboarding SKILL.md: jira.project_key → jira.project, design.system → design.system_name. Audit design.tokens_path in SKILL.md config table against validate-config.sh KNOWN_KEYS and resolve (add to KNOWN_KEYS or remove from table). Verify with codebase-wide grep that no files still reference the old names.
**Why**: SKILL.md references jira.project_key and design.system but KNOWN_KEYS uses jira.project and design.system_name — onboarding generates config keys that fail validation.
**Scope**:
- IN: Rename jira.project_key → jira.project in SKILL.md (lines 876, 1109); rename design.system → design.system_name in SKILL.md (line 1110); resolve design.tokens_path gap; codebase-wide grep audit for old key names
- OUT: design.* keys beyond the rename (handled by /dso:design-onboarding); changes to validate-config.sh key structure

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, grep -r "jira.project_key" and grep -r "design\.system[^_]" across the repo both return 0 matches
  ← Satisfies: "SC5 — key name mismatch correction"
- When this story is complete, design.tokens_path is either present in validate-config.sh KNOWN_KEYS or removed from SKILL.md config table
  ← Satisfies: "SC5 — design.tokens_path audit"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Key renames may appear in downstream skill files beyond onboarding — codebase-wide grep required before rename
- [Maintainability] All SKILL.md stories modify the same file — sequential execution recommended

comments.body: Fixed: All implementation tasks closed, completion-verifier PASS
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
